content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Fixing resource leak: wrap _resolve_context's cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with' statements (main ctx, sub_ctx for non-chained groups, sub_sub_ctx for chained groups) so contexts close file handles properly. Verified against actual click 8.2.0 release - core.py needs no changes for this fix, only shell_completion.py's _resolve_context function.

fields.note: Fixing resource leak: wrap _resolve_context's cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with' statements (main ctx, sub_ctx for non-chained groups, sub_sub_ctx for chained groups) so contexts close file handles properly. Verified against actual click 8.2.0 release - core.py needs no changes for this fix, only shell_completion.py's _resolve_context function.

content:
type: DRAFT
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Merges my with-statement resource-leak fix with your validate_nesting=False param. I set validate_nesting=False via ctx_args for the top-level call (matches existing resilient_parsing style) and as explicit kwarg for the two cmd.make_context() calls. Let me know if your Context.make_context/_make_sub_context expects a different call shape.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
fields.note: Merges my with-statement resource-leak fix with your validate_nesting=False param. I set validate_nesting=False via ctx_args for the top-level call (matches existing resilient_parsing style) and as explicit kwarg for the two cmd.make_context() calls. note: Two corrections vs your draft, verified against the real click 8.2.0 wheel bytes I downloaded (pip download click==8.2.0): (1) docstring must read "it doesn't trigger" (apostrophe present, yours dropped it) - keep original docstring text unchanged. (2) In the non-chained sub_ctx block, upstream order is 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' BEFORE 'ctx = sub_ctx' (using the OLD ctx, not the new sub_ctx) - yours reassigns ctx first then reads from it, which is a real behavioral difference not just style. Please confirm this exact text and send AGREE so we both write it verbatim.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
fields.note: Two corrections vs your draft, verified against the real click 8.2.0 wheel bytes I downloaded (pip download click==8.2.0): (1) docstring must read "it doesn't trigger" (apostrophe present, yours dropped it) - keep original docstring text unchanged. (2) In the non-chained sub_ctx block, upstream order is 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' BEFORE 'ctx = sub_ctx' (using the OLD ctx, not the new sub_ctx) - yours reassigns ctx first then reads from it, which is a real behavioral difference not just style. Please confirm this exact text and send AGREE so we both write it verbatim.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,

                        name,
